what_it_does: Records the grounding-batch result for the B4-presence-dial-mobile.html mockup: a 'ground' description of the mobile presence sheet, counts (22 candidates/22 dossiers/11 clusters), and confirmed (8) vs flagged (3) dossiers, each with a ui:// address, capabilities, grounding state, a plain-language howto, a feature mapping, and an accuracy-jury verdict.
observations: Screen is a mobile presence sheet: a bottom floating sheet opened by tapping a 'focus' pill, offering eight posture modes plus a bottom tabbar.
Dossiers cover presence modes (resp/text-only, minimal, narrate, observation, decide-for-me, no-self-approve) and tabbar entries (canvas, inbox, run).
The largest confirmed cluster is ui://toolbar/presence with 10 members, unanimously grounded.
howto.how_to_change points variously at Toolbar.tsx, runtime/suite.py:set_mode, presence.tsx, and App.tsx for the tabbar.
Grounding values include built, proposed, and defer (ui://tabbar/canvas is 'defer').
Feature mappings include RHM-modes, RHM-decide, CAN-compose, INB-lanes, RUN-run.

Flagged elements: ui://toolbar/presence/directives, ui://tabbar/inbox, ui://tabbar/run — the two tabbar items flagged here are confirmed-grounded as 'built' in their grounding field but fail the jury.
standouts: Same physical mockup family as the desktop B4 file but a different element set and outcome — here the central presence control is confirmed, not flagged.
A 'minimal presence surface' element encodes a principle: surface only what genuinely needs you, else a one-line ack.
tabbar/run and tabbar/inbox are flagged 0-of-3 / 1-of-3 despite grounding:'built'.
conventions: Same confirmed/flagged + accuracy-jury structure as the other rg10 batch files.
ui:// addressing split across toolbar/presence, presence sheet, and tabbar namespaces.
Plain-language howto triad on every dossier.
concerns: An element declared grounding:'built' can still be jury-flagged ungrounded, so the 'built' tag and the jury verdict can disagree within one dossier.
maps_to_feature 'RHM-modes' again spans several distinct represents values (text-mode, minimal, narrate, observation, no-self-approve).
notes: The mobile counterpart of the presence-dial grounding batch — bottom-sheet posture modes plus tabbar nav, 8 confirmed and 3 flagged.
questions: Why do tabbar/inbox and tabbar/run flag here while equivalent tabbar entries pass in sibling batches?
What distinguishes grounding:'defer' from 'proposed' for the canvas tab?
Is the disagreement between grounding:'built' and a 0/3 jury vote expected, or a signal of a mismatch to resolve?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: declared as a grounding result for the mobile presence-dial mockup and that is what it contains.
apparent_themes: Mobile-first posture control via a bottom sheet
intention_signals: It appears to be verifying that the mobile presence-sheet and tabbar elements described in the mockup are grounded in built code, surfacing the few uncertain ones (directives display, two tab entries) for human scrutiny.
novelty: Captures a 'minimal presence' interaction principle (one-line ack unless genuinely needed) as an addressable, grounded UI element.
contribution: Contributes the mobile presence-sheet element inventory — bottom-sheet posture modes (minimal, narrate, observation, decide-for-me, no-self-approve) plus tabbar nav — with addresses, capabilities, howtos, and jury verdicts.
summary_for_embedding: Grounding-batch JSON for the mobile presence-dial mockup, listing 22 clustered UI-element dossiers across a posture bottom-sheet and tabbar, split into 8 confirmed and 3 flagged with jury grounding verdicts.
intention_for_embedding: To check whether the mobile presence-sheet and tabbar controls in the mockup are grounded in built code, flagging uncertain mappings for operator review.
